title: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter, 1447–50
summary: The checked-in CME00024 XML is a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of Stuart A. Moore's 1871 Camden Society edited selection, not a transcription of one medieval codex or of the complete surviving Shillingford archive. It encodes the edition title page and the documentary text on printed pp. 1–154, arranged as 43 top-level document containers in three parts, while omitting the Camden preliminaries, Moore's introduction pp. i–xxvi, and the index pp. 155–161. Moore selected, transcribed, and sometimes conflated drafts, copies, fragments, and rolls now principally cataloged at Devon Heritage Centre as ECA/1/1/6; his appendix also excerpts ECA/11/MR/3 membrane 6, ECA/2/1/1/156 membrane 21, and six receiver's account rolls ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104. A complete public scan of the printed book and a complete supporting thesis are cached. No public complete digital surrogate of any of the four archival source groups was found on 2026-07-12; exact catalog, onsite, and mediated whole-object reproduction routes are recorded instead.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is Moore's 1871 edited documentary selection; the many historical documents, archival groupings, printed edition, U-M encoding, repository artifact, and scan remain separate layers.
The current ECA/1/1/6 child descriptions contain overlapping and missing document-number ranges, so they do not support an item-by-item crosswalk to Moore's printed numerals.
Complete-facsimile negatives refer only to public web access checked on 2026-07-12; the Devon catalog marks the originals open and the repository offers paid copying and permission routes.

The Internet Archive PDF is complete relative to the provider's exposed bound-volume access object; its scan-data inventory has 206 capture leaves but the PDF has 200 included pages, with front and rear boards and blanks but no separate spine or edge views.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00024
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 341,378 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.

Its TEMPHEAD records a review dated 2016-08-25 that added TEMPHEAD, proofed the title page, added previously missing beginning text keyed as M224, revised structure and note placement, and corrected page milestones; this does not establish the initial encoding date.
The representation contains 5 DIV1, 43 DIV2, 50 DIV3, 155 PB, 268 NOTE, and 222 P elements.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00024
entities.description: A public structured digital text of Moore's selected documents, with page-oriented navigation and edition-derived headings and notes.

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD date documents later review and correction activity, not original keying or publication.

notes: The XML declares TEXT/@LANG as enm, but the encoded edition also contains substantive Latin bonds and receiver-account extracts plus modern English editorial matter.
It encodes the edition title page and printed pp. 1–154, including Part I, Part II, and the appendix, but omits the contents, Moore's introduction pp. i–xxvi, and index pp. 155–161.
The 43 DIV2 containers are encoding and editorial-unit metrics; they are not a count of independent physical documents.

entities.label: Stuart A. Moore's 1871 Letters and Papers of John Shillingford
entities.description: Moore's edited selection and arrangement of letters, petitions, pleadings, agreements, and municipal-account extracts concerning Exeter's civic and cathedral jurisdictional dispute.

bibliographic.citation: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter 1447–50, ed. Stuart A. Moore, Camden Society, New Series II ([Westminster]: Printed for the Camden Society, 1871).
bibliographic.title: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter 1447–50

notes: The title page says printed for the Camden Society in 1871; the verso identifies J. B. Nichols and Sons, Westminster, as printer.
Moore says the papers had been dispersed in Exeter's municipal archives and thanks the Mayor and Corporation for lending the originals.
The edition prints 43 top-level documentary units in three parts, but its Roman numerals omit XXIV, duplicate XXXV, and restart later sequences incorrectly.
Moore sometimes constructs one reading from several drafts, copies, fragments, or rolls and records only some variants and interventions in footnotes.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Moore's 1871 volume
entities.description: A complete public access scan of the bound printed volume contributed by University of California Libraries and digitized by Internet Archive in 2008; it is not a facsimile of the medieval archival documents.
entities.reproduction_format: 200-page color Text PDF derived from a 400 ppi capture; exposed pages include front and rear boards, endleaves or blanks, preliminaries, pp. 1–161, and final blanks, without separate spine or edge views
notes: The scan-data file records 206 capture leaves and 400 dpi; the access PDF contains 200 pages because leaves marked out of access formats are omitted.
Visual inspection confirmed the red front and rear boards, edition title page, ordinary text, work ending, index, and final blank.

entities.label: Devon Heritage Centre ECA/1/1/6, Shillingford letters and papers
alternate_labels: C.S. Boxes 1859–1861 and associated flat folder
entities.description: An aggregate archival witness comprising many separate letters, petitions, drafts, fragments, and rolls concerning the dispute over St Stephen's or the Bishop's Fee; it is not one codex or one authored work.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.notes: This is the series-level repository date; individual documents in Moore's selection are more narrowly dated where evidence permits.

holding.notes: The current catalog divides the series into files ECA/1/1/6/1–4 at Devon Heritage Centre. Cruse used the older C.S. box and folder designations; an exact old-to-current and print-unit crosswalk has not been published.

physical_description.extent: 3 archive boxes and 1 oversize box in the current catalog; a historical study reports 76 documents ranging from small fragments to multi-membrane rolls
physical_description.format: dispersed archival documents, drafts, fragments, folders, and rolls
notes: Cruse emphasizes that the archive does not form one discrete manuscript.
The current four file descriptions list numbered documents with gaps and an overlap at nos. 72–73, plus unnumbered drafts and fragments; they cannot be treated as a settled 76-item inventory.
The current series record explicitly says that this material was printed in part in Moore's 1871 volume.
Moore's title and the archive's historical numbering do not prove that each printed unit equals one current physical document.

entities.label: Devon Heritage Centre ECA/11/MR/3, disputes over the Exe and Exbridge
entities.description: A 17-membrane archival file containing several distinct records about Exeter's disputes with the earls of Devon; Moore excerpted the John Shillingford Exbridge petition on membrane 6 as appendix item XL.

date_statements.label: file contents
date_statements.value: reigns of Edward I through Henry VI
date_statements.notes: The Shillingford petition on membrane 6 belongs after Cardinal Beaufort's death on 11 April 1447 and during John Kemp's archiepiscopate; exact composition date remains uncertain.

holding.notes: The CME00024 work portion is membrane 6 only; membranes 1–5 and 7–17 are adjacent records and must remain in any complete-object reproduction.

physical_description.format: archival roll or file of distinct documents
notes: The current item record catalogs each membrane's contents and marks the file open.

entities.label: Devon Heritage Centre ECA/2/1/1/156, Mayor's Court Roll, 23–24 Henry VI
entities.description: The complete 51-membrane mayoral court roll from which Moore prints an extract about Shillingford's election as appendix item XLI.

holding.notes: Moore identifies the selected passage as membrane 21; a complete surrogate must retain all 51 membranes and both sides where present.

entities.label: Devon Heritage Centre receiver's account rolls ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104
entities.description: Six consecutive Exeter receiver's account rolls from which Moore selected expense and gift entries for appendix item XLII.

holding.notes: The parent Account Rolls record supplies the format and print-reference note; six exact child records establish the work's source range.

physical_description.format: receiver's account rolls written on both sides and running Michaelmas to Michaelmas
notes: A complete reproduction must include every membrane and both sides of each of the six rolls, not only Moore's selected expense entries.

The current parent catalog says that large extracts from 1444–1450 appear on Moore pp. 143–152. Direct inspection instead finds six labeled rolls from 1443–1449 continuing through printed p. 154; the exact child records and primary scan control this manifest's range.

entities.label: Julia Cruse's 2014 scholarly description of the Shillingford archive and Moore edition
entities.description: A doctoral thesis based in part on physical examination and photography of the archival documents; it distinguishes the dispersed originals from Moore's printed conflations and documents numbering and editorial problems.

bibliographic.citation: Julia Cruse, Gentry Identity and the Politics of Vernacular Letter Writing in the Fifteenth Century (PhD thesis, University of Kent, 2014).
bibliographic.title: Gentry Identity and the Politics of Vernacular Letter Writing in the Fifteenth Century

locators: PDF pp. 79–84, section The Shillingford Letters and Papers
notes: Cruse surveys the then boxes and folder, documents Moore editorial conflation and numbering errors, and distinguishes the many physical documents from the printed collection.
The thesis is cached as a complete supporting scholarly source, not as a manuscript facsimile.

rights.component: The U-M digital collection item as presented
rights.basis: The exact U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States rights statement.

rights.component: The 1871 edition text, typography, and apparatus
rights.basis: U-M applies No Copyright–United States to the exact 1871 item, and Wikimedia Commons labels the Internet Archive-derived scan PD-US-expired.

rights.component: The public scan derivative of the 1871 volume
rights.basis: The Commons file page identifies this Internet Archive item and direct PDF as its source and marks the derivative PD-US-expired; the Internet Archive metadata itself supplies no rights or license field.
notes: The cached exact Internet Archive PDF and the separately hosted Commons PDF are not byte-identical, so the Commons label is preserved as a downstream assertion rather than an open license inferred for every IA file-layer asset.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A dispersed and incomplete archival group of many distinct physical documents, plus illustrative municipal rolls outside the principal Shillingford series
editorial_practices.representation: Three editorial parts containing 43 top-level documentary units on pp. 1–154
editorial_practices.description: Moore created an edited selection rather than publishing the complete archive, and he added appendix extracts from separately cataloged Exbridge, mayoral-court, and receiver-account rolls.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Originals, drafts, copies, incomplete rolls, fragments, deletions, additions, and alternative wording
editorial_practices.representation: Composite reading texts with square-bracket supplies and footnoted variants, often labeled A or B
editorial_practices.description: Moore sometimes conflated multiple physical witnesses or document states into one printed text. Cruse finds the practice and its documentation inconsistent and notes that not every manuscript alteration appears in print.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected Part I and Part II documents, including items IV, XXXI, and XXXVI

editorial_practices.source_feature: Forty-three distinct printed containers in a three-part arrangement
editorial_practices.representation: Nonsequential Roman numerals with omitted XXIV, duplicate XXXV, and later renumbering errors
editorial_practices.description: The printed numerals are inherited labels, not a reliable consecutive count or physical-document inventory; the XML preserves them while its DIV2 count remains 43.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed contents and document headings across pp. 1–154

editorial_practices.source_feature: A printed volume with title and society preliminaries, contents, introduction, documentary text, and index
editorial_practices.representation: Edition title page and documentary pp. 1–154 encoded; contents, introduction pp. i–xxvi, index pp. 155–161, and binding matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text's extent is narrower than the complete printed edition even though it carries page breaks through the documentary ending.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A pre-existing structured transcription with missing opening text, redundant text, and inconsistent structural and note tagging
editorial_practices.representation: Opening text added from separately keyed M224 material; redundant matter removed; divisions, notes, closers, arguments, page breaks, and other tags revised
editorial_practices.description: The TEMPHEAD documents a substantial 2016 review but provides no complete initial keying or OCR history.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR; EEBO/IDG; edition title page; BODY DIV1 parts; printed-page PB milestones; document heads, footnotes, opening and closing matter
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00024 and Moore's 1871 edition; preserves the title page and pp. 1–154 in 43 DIV2 containers; documents languages in practice, editorial notes and composite readings, appendix sources, and the 2016 review.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, 'Letters and papers of John Shillingford, mayor of Exeter 1447-50,' CME00024.
evidence.locator: About this Item; title, author, publication, rights, subject terms, stable link, and full-text page navigation
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact item title, John Shillingford author heading, Moore responsibility, Westminster/Camden Society 1871 publication, public full-text access, subjects, stable CME identifier, public-domain statement, and No Copyright–United States label.

evidence.citation: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter 1447–50, ed. Stuart A. Moore, Camden Society New Series II (1871), Internet Archive letterspapersofj00shilrich.
evidence.locator: Title and imprint leaves; contents pp. v–xii; documentary pp. 1–154; index pp. 155–161
evidence.summary: Provides the complete edition for bibliographic verification, exact inclusion and omission mapping, printed headings and dates, footnoted collation practices, 43 documentary containers, appendix source locators, and documentary ending.

evidence.citation: Stuart A. Moore, introduction to Letters and Papers of John Shillingford (1871), pp. xiii–xxvi.
evidence.summary: States that the papers were found dispersed in Exeter's corporate archives, describes their incomplete survival and the jurisdictional dispute, identifies letters, pleadings, and account extracts, comments on draft and fair-copy variants, and thanks Exeter's corporation for lending the originals.

Identifies the University of California Libraries contribution, 1871 publication, public item, 2008 scan, exact PDF filename and size, and downloadable scan-data and OCR support files; its rights and license fields are empty.
notes: The SHA-256 identifies the cached JSON snapshot downloaded on 2026-07-12.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan-data XML for letterspapersofj00shilrich.
evidence.locator: bookData leafCount and pageNumData; pageData leaves 1–205
evidence.summary: Records a 206-leaf, 400 dpi capture; maps printed p. 1 to BookReader leaf 33 and printed p. 154 to leaf 186; identifies covers, title, contents, blanks, and access-format inclusion flags.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the cached Internet Archive PDF for Moore's 1871 volume.
evidence.summary: Representative pages confirm a legible color capture with red boards and cream leaves, the correct title page, contents and introduction, work opening and ending, index, rear board, and final blank; no separate spine or edge image is present.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons file page for the Internet Archive-derived Letters and Papers of John Shillingford PDF.
evidence.summary: Identifies Internet Archive item letterspapersofj00shilrich and its direct PDF as the file source and applies the PD-US-expired template; the Commons-hosted PDF is a distinct downstream derivative.

evidence.citation: Royal Historical Society, Camden New Series (1871–1901), volume list.
evidence.summary: Lists Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter, 1447–1450, edited by Stuart A. Moore, as Camden Society New Series volume II and dates it 1871.

evidence.citation: Devon Archives and Local Studies, ECA/1/1/6, 'Shillingford letters and papers,' and child files ECA/1/1/6/1–4.
evidence.locator: Series description, extent, access status, publication note, and four child-file descriptions
evidence.summary: Locates the principal archive at Devon Heritage Centre, dates it broadly to the fifteenth century, gives an extent of three archive boxes and one oversize box, marks it Open, says Moore printed it in part, and supplies current component and previous references. The child number ranges contain gaps and overlap and therefore do not settle an item-level crosswalk.

evidence.citation: Devon Archives and Local Studies, ECA/11/MR/3, 'Disputes between the city of Exeter and the Earls of Devon.'
evidence.summary: Catalogs a 17-membrane open file and identifies membrane 6 as John Shillingford's petition to Archbishop John Kemp for assistance rebuilding Exbridge; the other membranes contain adjacent and unrelated Exe-dispute records.

evidence.citation: Devon Archives and Local Studies, ECA/2/1/1/156, 'Mayor's Court Roll, 23–24 Hen. VI.'
evidence.summary: Confirms the current exact shelfmark, 1444–1445 date, one-roll extent of 51 membranes, Devon Heritage Centre holding, and Open status for Moore's appendix XLI source roll.

evidence.citation: Devon Archives and Local Studies, ECA/3/1/1/1, Account Rolls, especially ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104.
evidence.locator: Parent description, extent, Open status, Copies note, and child records /99–104
evidence.summary: Describes Exeter's receivers' rolls as double-sided annual rolls, explicitly notes large extracts in Shillingford, and identifies the six exact consecutive rolls dated 1443–1444 through 1448–1449; none of the parent or child records exposes digitisation.
notes: The parent copy note's 1444–1450 and pp. 143–152 wording conflicts with the inspected edition's labels and extent; those catalog details are retained as attributed evidence rather than silently adopted.

evidence.citation: South West Heritage Trust, Devon Archives and Local Studies, research, copying, photography, charges, and booking guidance.
evidence.locator: Copyright; camera licences; research and copying; permission to publish; booking and document ordering
evidence.summary: Confirms onsite access and document ordering, paid copying and private-camera routes, private research restrictions on copies, and a separate written-permission and fee process for publication or online use.

evidence.citation: Julia Cruse, Gentry Identity and the Politics of Vernacular Letter Writing in the Fifteenth Century (PhD thesis, University of Kent, 2014).
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 79–84, especially The Shillingford Letters and Papers and The nineteenth-century edition
evidence.summary: Reports the historical box and folder arrangement, a lost 76-document inventory, paper and parchment supports ranging from fragments to rolls, Moore's selection and nonsequential numbering, conflation of drafts and copies, inconsistent recording of manuscript changes, and the absence of one discrete Shillingford manuscript.

evidence.citation: Public complete-facsimile search for CME00024 archival sources, 2026-07-12.
evidence.summary: Checks of the current Devon series and exact component records, their digitisation and attachment fields, the U-M CME item, Internet Archive, Wikimedia Commons, and the Kent thesis found a complete scan of the print edition and one selected thesis illustration but no public complete digital surrogate for ECA/1/1/6, ECA/11/MR/3, ECA/2/1/1/156, or ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104. Every exact Devon record checked exposed no digitisation or public attachments.

open_questions.question: How do Moore's 43 printed documentary containers and source labels map item by item to current ECA/1/1/6 files /1–/4 and their older box, folder, and ECA/MR designations?
next_steps: Ask Devon Archives whether an internal item list or old-to-current concordance survives.
Compare every printed opening, endorsement, material note, and variant siglum against the originals under current references.
Reconcile the child-record gaps and overlap in document numbers before assigning item-level shelfmarks.

open_questions.question: Can Devon Archives supply condition-safe complete digital surrogates of all four physical source groups, including all adjacent and non-work material?
next_steps: Request quotations and condition assessments for complete ECA/1/1/6, all 17 membranes of ECA/11/MR/3, all 51 membranes of ECA/2/1/1/156, and every surface of ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104.
Keep private research-copy permission separate from publication and redistribution permission.
If supplied, map CME00024 portions separately and retain full-object coverage in the private cache.

open_questions.question: When and by what process was the U-M CME00024 transcription first created before the documented 2016 review?
next_steps: Consult U-M collection production records or archived item metadata.
Do not infer manual keying, OCR, or initial publication date from the later TEMPHEAD review.

open_questions.question: Why does Moore's title use 1447–50 when the encoded and inspected documentary text labels account material through 1448–49 and concentrates the correspondence in 1447–48?
next_steps: Treat 1447–50 as part of the established title rather than silently as the exact date range of every selected document.
Check a future item-level archive concordance and Moore's unencoded source notes for any intended 1450 endpoint.
